What just happened — accepted record

what-just-happened
Initiative
initiative-slug
Status
approved
Source revision
not available

Acta v0.2.0-pilot record · Generated from accepted canonical state (incidents/INC-0000.md) after explicit acceptance (chat — postmortem reviewed after stabilization) · Never generated from browser state.

dossier record · ACCEPTED

Incident record

One paragraph: impact, cause, mitigation, durable fix.

SEV-3resolvedduration NN mindetected date · time

TL;DR

What happened

Three sentences: trigger, impact, mitigation, durable fix — no mystery left.

its valuea real impact measure

Timeline

Entries are typed: fact (directly supported), hypothesis (kept even when falsified), mitigation, recovery.

  1. 00:00fact

    Impact starts

    What was observed, from evidence.

  2. 00:10hypothesis

    First hypothesis

    Kept even when falsified, with how it was tested.

  3. 00:20mitigation

    Mitigated

    The step that stopped the bleeding.

  4. laterrecovery

    Durable fix

    The change that removes the cause.

Root cause

The mechanism, stated causally, with the condition that let it ship.

path/file.mjs @ incident revision
// the code state that produced the incident
function example() {}

Contributing conditions

  • a contributing condition distinct from the cause

Follow-ups

  • ○open: a follow-up without an invented owner

Sources

  1. [E1] the chat log, alert history, or deploy log the timeline derives from

Acta is a print-first engineering record. Fact = directly supported; Inference = reasoned from evidence; Simplification = deliberately reduced model. This record renders accepted canonical state only; open decisions live in the companion instrument.
